role: First head of IRIB (1979–1980); Foreign minister (1979–1980)
bio: Sadegh Ghotbzadeh was a member of the [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran) and a close associate of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) during the exile years in Paris. He played a key role in the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979). He briefly served as the first head of [IRIB](/orgs/irib) in 1979, overseeing the transformation of the Shah-era National Iranian Radio and Television into the revolutionary broadcasting apparatus. He was subsequently appointed foreign minister, serving during the [Iran hostage crisis](/events/iran-hostage-crisis-1979) and the early months of the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war). Ghotbzadeh grew disillusioned with the clerical monopoly on power and was arrested in 1982 on charges of plotting a coup against [Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) with the alleged involvement of Ayatollah Shariatmadari. He was executed by firing squad on 15 September 1982.
editorial_notes: One of the few revolutionary figures executed by the regime he helped create. His trial was broadcast on IRIB.
